emails.subject: ETA
emails.body: Hello Mark, Please advise me on this matter and let me know if you want me to follow up with the customer or if you contact customer. EOL customer is inquiring about if their Lawyers can modify or make comments to the ETA. Thanks. Cecil John EnronOnline 713-853-6259 Forwarded by Cecil John/Corp/Enron on 01/24/2000 02:33 PM "Smith MD (Mark)" <MDSmith@equiva.com> on 01/24/2000 02:01:11 PM

Please let me know, how you would like to respond to customer's inquiry about modifications to the ETA.. Thanks. emails.subject: Re: ETA
emails.body: Re: ETA Sorry for the delay our general policy is (and the first response to this type of question should be) that due to system requirements, it is not possible to provide for different online contracts for different counterparties. Having said that, in the few cases where a counterparty has raised what we consider to be valid and material concerns, we have agreed to a side letter agreement with that particular counterparty to vary the terms of the ETA. So if the counterparty resist the first response and threatens to not use the system for legal reasons, you should refer them to the legal department. They can contact either Carol St. Clair (x33989) or me (x37459). Cecil John@ENRON 01/27/2000 11:00 AM

They can contact either Carol St. Clair (x33989) or me (x37459). Cecil John@ENRON 01/27/2000 11:00 AM
